humanities_role_explanation: Based on digitized historical Czech books and periodicals (content) and specifically designed to assist digital humanities applications such as thematic tracing and archival exploration by historians (domain).
benchmark_modalities_explanation: Monolingual textual data consisting of segments from OCR-processed historical Czech documents.
analytical_tasks_explanation: Evaluating the detection of topic presence at the document level and the precise word-level identification of overlapping or non-contiguous text spans that express a predefined topic's name and description.

general_purpose_benchmark.note: It is a specialized domain benchmark focusing specifically on topic localization in historical Czech texts.

evaluates_generative_llm.note: Evaluated multiple LLMs including GPT-5 (2, mini, and nano variants), Llama 3.3, Llama 3.2, Gemma 3, Gemini 3 Pro, and gpt-oss-20b.

llm_is_primary_system.note: Generative LLMs are benchmarked as primary systems alongside fine-tuned BERT-based models.

quantitative_metrics.note: Precision, Recall, F1-score (at text and word level), Intersection over Union (IoU), and localization success rate.

english_language.note: The scientific paper is written in English, though the benchmark dataset is in Czech.

summary: This paper presents CzechTopic, a novel human-annotated benchmark for zero-shot topic localization using historical Czech archives. It introduces a word-level span localization task evaluated against human agreement and benchmarks several state-of-the-art LLMs as well as BERT-based cross-encoders fine-tuned on a distilled dataset.
key_findings: The best-performing LLM (GPT-5-2) achieved a word-level F1 of 61.1 and IoU of 48.7, remaining significantly below the human baseline (68.7 F1, 57.2 IoU). Fine-tuned BERT cross-encoders (e.g., robeczech at 48.3 F1) performed competitively, outperforming several larger generative LLMs.
